name: Foudre Friday Apero League Race
description:
🚴 Course Hebdomadaire Team Foudre

    Quand : Tous les Vendredis soirs.
    Heure de départ : 19h15 (France/CET), soit 18h15 (UK/GMT ou BST).
    Objectif : S'amuser et s'affronter.
    Format : Chaque semaine, un parcours différent.
    Ligue : Les courses comptent pour notre ligue du mois (composée de 4 ou 5 courses).
        Précision : Seules les 3 meilleures courses de chaque coureur seront retenues pour le classement mensuel de la ligue.

🏆 Classements et Points (Course au Scratch)

    Classements : Un classement individuel et un classement par équipe.
    Système de Points (Arrivée) :
        1er : 100 points
        2e : 99 points
        3e : 98 points
        ... et ainsi de suite jusque 1 point.
    Période ZRL : Des reconnaissances des parcours sont organisées durant la période ZRL 

🔗 Comment nous rejoindre et obtenir plus d'infos

    Planning Mensuel : Sur nos pages Facebook :
        https://www.facebook.com/groups/foudre.friday.apero.league
        https://www.facebook.com/groups/180845029459880
    Toutes les Infos Utiles : Via notre serveur Discord, notamment dans les salons publics de l'équipe :
        https://discord.gg/YzcVYJY



Et en anglais:




🚴 Team Foudre's Friday Night Race
Team Foudre invites you to come have fun and compete in our Friday night race! ⚡️
Here is a summary of the key points for our event:
📅 Weekly Race Details

    When: Every Friday night.
    Start Time: 7:15 PM (France/CET), which is 6:15 PM (UK/GMT or BST).
    Goal: To have fun and compete.
    Format: A different course every week.
    League: The races count towards our monthly league (consisting of 4 or 5 races).
        Important Note: Only the best 3 races of each rider will be retained for the monthly league ranking.

🏆 Standings and Points (Scratch Race)

    Standings: An individual ranking and a team ranking.
    Point System (Finish):
        1st: 100 points
        2nd: 99 points
        3rd: 98 points
        ... and so on down to 1 point.
    ZRL Period: Course recons (reconnaissance rides) are organized during the ZRL period.

🔗 How to Join Us and Get More Info

    Monthly Schedule: On our Facebook pages:
        https://www.facebook.com/groups/foudre.friday.apero.league
        https://www.facebook.com/groups/180845029459880
    All Useful Info: Via our Discord server, particularly in the team's public channels:
        https://discord.gg/YzcVYJY

name: ZRG-CC Social Ride
description:
Zwift Riders Germany social ride Please observe the following rules: The speed is around 1,5W/Kg. Please make sure that you do not use a TT-bike, only then you can enjoy the draft shade within the large group. This ride is not a race. Please stay close to the leader (yellow mark). The end of the field is marked by the sweeper (red mark). You can find more information about the group trip in our Facebook Group at https://www.facebook.com/ZwiftRidersGermany/ or on Strava at https://www.strava.com/clubs/zwift-riders-germany

Zwift Riders Germany social ride Beachtet bitte folgende Regeln: Das Tempo liegt bei  1,5W/Kg. Bitte achte darauf, dass du kein TT-Bike verwendest, nur dann kommst du in den Genuss des Windschattens innerhalb der großen Gruppe. Diese Fahrt ist kein Rennen. Bitte halte dich in der Nähe des Leaders (gelbe Markierung) auf. Das Ende des Feldes wird vom Sweeper (rote Markierung) gekennzeichnet. Weitere Informationen zur Gruppenfahrt findest du in unserer Facebook Gruppe unter https://www.facebook.com/ZwiftRidersGermany/ oder auf Strava unter https://www.strava.com/clubs/zwift-riders-germany

#zrg

name: Mountain Massif structured training: Power Climbs
description:
Created by Matt Rowe, from Rowe & King Coaching, this structured training session is designed to help you cycle at a higher intensity – essential attributes for any Zwift racer.   

For coaching and all your training needs visit https://roweandking.com/


Session details:
Warmup:
• 10-minute warmup (2-minutes gradual increase)

Interval Set x 5:
• 10-seconds @ 150% FTP
• 1.50-minutes @ 90% FTP

Interval Set x 4:
• 60-seconds @ 115% FTP
• 2-minutes @ 65% FTP

Interval Set x 1:
• 60-seconds @ 130% FTP

Cool down:
• 5-minute cool down

Safety Note:
Only undertake vigorous exercise if you are fit and healthy to do so. If you are at all unsure, consult a Physician or Doctor first.  All training equipment including Bikes and Turbo trainers should be in full working order. Do not use any piece of equipment that does not come up to the required standard.


More information:
For more information check out the Mountain Massif website www.mountainmassif.com, follow on Facebook, Instagram (Mountain_Massif) and join our Zwift Club by clicking the following link https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b639575d-930f-4de6-a79d-bea3313350bc/join.  To record all your training data and chart your progress, consider using the TrainingPeaks app (www.trainingpeaks.com/).

eventSubgroups.name: Mountain Massif structured training: Power Climbs (E)

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Champs Elysees
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: PACK🐺 1.5 Weekly Wind Down
description:
This PACK 1.5 beginner friendly event is aimed at Zwifters looking for a group ride experience at an average lap pace of around 1.3-1.5w/kg.

On the flat, the pace will be controlled to ensure the group draft effect is kept under tight control with a target of 1.5w/kg on any hills or upslopes. The Leader will text instructions as well as use discord voice chat as communication is the key to a good group ride. Sweepers will also be in operation to assist in helping the group stay together as this is a non-drop ride.

All riders will appear in the same kit and you can optionally add (PACK) after your name to show support of the group. Please respect the pace set by the leader, this is a group ride not a race. #strongertogether

Finally, if you are just starting your zwift experience, please join our facebook group and PACK Club for tips on group riding, friendly advice and to answer any questions you have.

PACK Club:
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/Pack/join

Facebook Group #zwiftpack
https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftpack/

PACK Events:
https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/pack

Discord Voice Chat:
https://discord.gg/cce7ers

Zwiftpower Team:
https://www.zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=2928

name: GXY SHOOTING STARS [2.8-3.2 wkg] CAT C
description:
You are blasting your way through the Zwift Cosmos at around 3.0 w/kg for 75 minutes – right at the top end of Cat C.

This ride is designed for strong C-riders who are comfortable riding hard and holding wheels.

The mission: keep the Shooting Star intact as we travel through the Galaxy.

⚠️ This is an unswept ride – there are no sweepers. Once you lose the Meteor’s tail, space gets very cold very fast. Ride smart, stay together and help each other keep the group intact.

On the climbs, expect short warp-speed surges up to ~4.0 w/kg to keep the group moving and avoid splits.

Let’s have some fun, stay in formation and enjoy the ride as we blast through the Galaxy.

(Power suggestions based on a 75kgs rider.)

If you want to become a member and join our community to race, ride socially, or both, please come and join ideally via our Facebook link and clubs feature,

or by the email below:

https://www.facebook.com/groups/galaxycyclingclub/

https://www.strava.com/clubs/989894

https://www.instagram.com/galaxycyclingclub

eventSubgroups.name: GXY SHOOTING STARS [2.8-3.2 wkg] CAT C (C)
eventSubgroups.description:
You are blasting our way through the Zwift Cosmos at somewhere around 3.0 wkg for 60 mins.
You will join other like minded riders whose aim will be to keep the Shooting Star in tact.
Anyone falling off the Meteor’s tail will wherever possible be brought back into the purple cigar shaped Meteoroid.
Let’s have some fun, riding off into the Galaxy before we crash back down to Earth after 60 mins just in time to get the weekend started!

name: ZZRC The Weekend Starts Here
description:
Welcome to our ZZRC Weekend Starts Here
2-2.5.This is a group ride, not a race, where we encourage riding together.

Our rides are open to all riders, the category enforcement merely allows us to ensure a safe and quality ride for you the riders and for our group.

In 2015 ZZRC was founded and are one of the oldest established clubs on Zwift .

YELLOW BEACON will pace between 2-2.5 on the ride , with a possible increase of watts on inclines or sprints , but the average for the whole ride will be as advertised.

We strongly promote group riding so on most of our rides we will use the Fence to help encourage that. Using a fence ensures that the ride is not over paced .

In the event of falling off the back of the main group , you will need to pace well over group pace to bridge the gap and catch .

This is where our awesome ZZRC sweeps and Ride Buddies will help you return to group using their draft. They will give you clear encouraging instructions to aid this process .

Our crew are a caring bunch who dedicate a lot of hours to this club , but want you to have the best ride experience on Zwift

You will be fully supported and encouraged throughout the ride by the Leader, Sweeps and Ride Buddies .

Our rides do not allow the use of TT Bikes as they do not draft and will require you to push harder on the ride, and in these instances our Sweeps will not be able to help you .

Mountain bikes operate very similarly, minimum draft so more watts (effort) will be required for the ride and any chase backs to group .

All our rides have a 30 minute late join feature which allows you to rejoin if you have technical issues or you find yourself out of the group in the early stages of the ride. Using this feature SHOULD teleport you back into the main group with our YELLOW BEACON .


We love to have a chat on our rides so join us on our dedicated discord channel
https://discord.gg/QdeS3PtXTX


TOP TIPS

1) Stay close to the ride leader (or just in front on climbs), don’t let a gap form and the elastic snap!

2) Stay away from the Fence – We don’t like to fry anyone

3) Most important of all . . . HAVE FUN AND ENJOY THE RIDE.

#zzrc

Don’t forget to join our ZZRC-We Pace Not Race Facebook page and our club on the companion app

name: Het is Koers Weekend Starter !
description:
Het is Koers, the famous Dutch cycling blog, presents you a variety of well guided rides. On Friday evening we make a party to start the weekend.

Weekends starter (C, 2.3-2.8W/kg)
Between 1st Sunday of October until the last Sunday of March, we will offer you a C (2.3 - 2.8w/kg) category paced social but spicy ride. Your ride leader completes the ride with some sprints and/or climbs.
All riders are encouraged to keep the bunch together, so we can start and finish as one happy blob. Everybody is welcome as long as you make a positive contribution in making this a good and relaxed start of the weekend.
Please have a nice chat and ride, give your fellow riders a Ride On and share your beers/drinks after the ride on our HIK Facebook Group page.
Between 1st Sunday of April until last Sunday of September HIK schedules the C category rides as a free ride (no lead) on challenging routes. On weekly basis you can ride with other C category riders to start your weekend.

Weekends starter (D, 1.7-2.2W/kg)
All year round HIK schedules the D category rides as a free ride (so no lead) on mainly flat routes. On weekly basis you can ride with other D category riders to start your weekend.

Check out our weekly ride announcements on the HIK Facebook https://www.facebook.com/groups/hetiskoers
Join Het Is Koers on Zwift https://www.zwift.com/clubs/HetIsKoers/home
Other HIK events:
Endurance Recovery! (C) 2.2-2.6 W/kg, Saturday 9.00 AM CET
In the Dutch mountains (C) 2.5-3.0 W/kg, Sunday 9.00 AM CET
Watts More! (C) 2.5-3.0 W/kg, Tuesday 19.00 CET
Something Different! (D) 2.2-2.3 W/kg, Thursday 19.15 CET

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: Friday Night Fun Race
description:
Get ready for the Friday Night Fun Race – the perfect way to unwind and kick-off your weekend! This is fast, friendly, and totally unserious racing designed for pure enjoyment.

Every week brings a different course and theme, so you never know what fun-fueled surprise awaits! And if that wasn't enough, everyone starts with a 'BOOST' power-up, with extras available throughout the race – that’s a speed increase for a few seconds – use it wisely!

SCHEDULE
Races are held weekly on Friday evenings across various timezones.

CATEGORIES
You’ll be categorized by your existing Racing Score for fair competition. But don't worry – race results won't impact your score, keeping the pressure low and the fun high.

Sign up for the ultimate Friday night race – fun-filled, feel-good, and guaranteed to make your weekend start with a bang!

name: Zwift Epic Race - Downtown Dolphin
description:
Join us as we take on some slightly more challenging race routes!

For more Epic Races, please join the club on the Companion App: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/a1ea0324-7f4a-46a7-8547-a7a3246e349b/home

All racers will start together, so make sure you're ready at the start!

WHAT IS ZWIFTPOWER? 
For further information on connecting to ZwiftPower, check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note: For your results to show up in ZwiftPower, you must use a heart rate monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter. 

Check out zwift.com/racing for more information on how you can participate in other events offered by Zwift and the community to have a great racing experience.

#zwiftofficial

name: ZZRC Goat Airlines Flight Sub 2
description:
Welcome to our ZZRC Goat Airlines Sub 2. This is a group ride, not a race, where we encourage riding together.

Our rides are open to all riders, the category enforcement merely allows us to ensure a safe and quality ride for you the riders and for our group.

In 2015 ZZRC was founded and are one of the oldest established clubs on Zwift .

YELLOW BEACON will pace between 1.7-1.9 on the ride , with a possible increase of watts on inclines or sprints , but the average for the whole ride will be sub 2 .

We strongly promote group riding so on most of our rides we will use the Fence to help encourage that. Using a fence ensures that the ride is not over paced .

In the event of falling off the back of the main group , you will need to pace well over group pace to bridge the gap and catch .

This is where our awesome ZZRC sweeps and Ride Buddies will help you return to group using their draft. They will give you clear encouraging instructions to aid this process .

Our crew are a caring bunch who dedicated a lot of hours to this club , but want you to have the best ride experience on Zwift

You will be fully supported and encouraged throughout the ride by the Leader, Sweeps and Ride Buddies .

Our rides do not allow the use of TT Bikes as they do not draft and will require you to push harder on the ride, and in these instances our Sweeps will not be able to help you .

Mountain bikes operate very similarly, minimum draft so more watts (effort) will be required for the ride and any chase backs to group .

All our rides have a 30 minute late join feature which allows you to rejoin if you have technical issues or you find yourself out of the group in the early stages of the ride. Using this feature SHOULD teleport you back into the main group with our YELLOW BEACON .


We love to have a chat on our rides so join us on our dedicated discord channel for our sub 2
https://discord.gg/RJ3dhbjCEp


TOP TIPS

1) Stay close to the ride leader (or just in front on climbs), don’t let a gap form and the elastic snap!

2) Stay away from the Fence – We don’t like to fry anyone

3) Most important of all . . . HAVE FUN AND ENJOY THE RIDE.

#zzrc

Don’t forget to join our ZZRC-We Pace Not Race Facebook page and our club on the companion app

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Glasgow Crit Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Friday Fun - GTR Group Ride
description: Start your weekend with a friendly, 1-hour group ride with the Good Time Riders, team GTR. Get the legs spinning for an hour and work off a few calories before you hit the weekend hard. Our yellow ride leaders will aim to ride between 2.0 and 2.3 w/kg, with our red beacons helping to keep the group together at the rear.The fence will be switched on in front of the ride leader to encourge group riding at the advertised pace.

name: Flash Race - Team INOX
description:
⚡️🚴🏼‍💨 Flash Race by Team INOX ⚡️🚴🏼‍💨
• You must have an active Zwift Power account on https://zwiftpower.com
• Heart rate monitors are mandatory for podium positions
• Smart trainers or power meters must be connected as the power source to participate in races
Make sure you meet all requirements to avoid disqualification

The Flash Race is an intensely competitive race on short route, tests pure cyclist power. Time trial bikes are allowed, enabling athletes to maximize aerodynamics.

Do you have what it takes to run a mile faster than FLASH? Test yourself 
We’re waiting for you every Friday. Daily results on Zwiftpower.

More info will be communicated promptly via the Discord and Facebook channels
The organizers reserve the right to carry out checks in case of suspected misconduct and if necessary cancel the performances obtained

CONTACT US
• Website: https://www.teaminox.it/
• Facebook Page: https://www.facebook.com/groups/inoxteam/
• Zwift Companion Club: https://www.zwift.com/eu/clubs/cef70cde...44703/home
• Discord Channel: https://discord.gg/cpr3rBGaZy
• ZwiftPower: https://zwiftpower.com/team.php?id=16461
• STRAVA: https://strava.app.link/p2Oj6TaE3Gb

Everyone is Welcome! 🙏🏻

name: EVOLVE 25km Road Race
description:
Join Team Evolve for the EVOLVE 25, an electrifying 25km race on Zwift, utilizing the Zwift Racing Score (ZRS) system to match cyclists of all levels in a dynamic, community-driven virtual peloton. This Category Enforced event requires a smart trainer/power meter and heart rate monitor, grouping riders by their ZRS (0-1000 scale), fostering a competitive yet fair
atmosphere.

ZRS FAQ: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

About Us
At Team Evolve, we are more than just a group of athletes we are a dynamic and inclusive community united by our passion for a wide range of activities, both virtual and outdoor. Whether you’re into structured training, social rides, running, swimming, or simply
staying active in any form, you’ll discover a welcoming and supportive environment here.

For more information visit our website www.teamevolve.cc and join us on our Discord Server: https://discord.gg/teamevolve

name: Team Italy Social SUB2 Ride
description:
WELCOME TO ALL RIDERS!
Team Italy Social SUB2 Ride is not a fast pace group ride or race.

RULES:
Please join us for our event for a pace between (1.8- 2.2 w/kg) for 60 mins.
The focus of the group ride is keeping the group together the entire ride.
Follow the ride Leader (yellow beacon) instructions at all times, the fence is the killer one, so if you don't respect the leader pace, it will kick you out, our advice is to join the sweeper behind the group if you have extra watts.

Staying in the group draft is very important.
Your jersey will be autoselected on the Zwift Christmas Kit .
Please, add tag [ITA] after your name.

SPRINTS:
Regroup right after optional sprints.
This is the Facebook group of Team Italy https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftitaly/
and Strava Club of Team Italy https://www.strava.com/clubs/zwift_italy
Join us! Have fun! Ride On!

-
BENVENUTO A TUTTI I RIDERS!
Team Italy Social SUB2 Ride non è una gara ma una pedalata di gruppo.

REGOLE:
Unitevi a noi per il nostro evento per un ritmo tra 1.8- 2.2w/kg per 60 minuti.
L'obiettivo della pedalata è mantenere il gruppo unito per tutta la durata dell'evento.
Segui sempre le istruzioni del Leader (segnale giallo), se andrete oltre la barriera rossa vuol dire che non state rispettando il passo del leader e verrete espulsi, il nostro consiglio è di aiutare lo sweeper, con la freccia rossa, se avete più ritmo.

Rimanere nella pancia di gruppo è molto importante, la scia è molto forte.
La tua maglia verrà autoselezionata sul kit Natalizio di Zwift.
Per favore, aggiungi il tag [ITA] dopo il tuo nome.

SPRINT:
attendere il Leader subito dopo gli sprint, che sono facoltativi.

Questo è il gruppo Facebook del Team Italy https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftitaly/
Strava Club del Team Italy https://www.strava.com/clubs/zwift_italy.

name: TNP The No Prisoners Power Test April 5 Minute Power Challenge Week 1
description:
This is a month long challenge running throughout April, designed to test one of the most important efforts in Zwift racing.
Three Sisters Reverse Race distance: 6.9 km (lead in 3.2 km, segment length 3.7 km)
Zwift races are often won or lost in efforts around five minutes. Short climbs, hard attacks, and decisive selections usually happen in that window. If you can produce strong power for five minutes, you can stay with the front when races split. This challenge is designed to test exactly that. Hit some of Zwift’s most iconic climbs full gas and see how your best time stacks up.
"No April fools here. Just power, passion and heart. Short lead in.
One climb.
Fastest time wins.

Full gas. No prisoners."
Results will be on https://team-not-pogi-hub.vercel.app/

description:
Welcome to the WCC Friday Fried Legs Recovery ride. Did you find you burnt all your matches on the TTT yesterday? Do you need a relaxing social recovery ride to work out the kinks that remain? Just looking for an enjoyable 60 minute chin wag? You have found the right place.

name: Mountain Massif Group Run
description:
Join Mountain Massif for some relaxed group runs. The goal is to simply complete the route and collect the badge.

More information:
For more information check out the Mountain Massif website www.mountainmassif.com, follow on Instagram, Mountain_Massif and join our Zwift Club, by clicking the link below
https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b639575d-930f-4de6-a79d-bea3313350bc/join

name: Sledgehammer Inbetweeners Races
description:
Welcome to the Sledgehammer series.
These races are designed to equalise the categories as much as possible and encourage team riding between riders of different strengths. Please enter your appropriate category according to your Zwiftpower race category.
All out of category entrants will be excluded from the Zwiftpower results.
Organiser's Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/groups/2388754431339748/
Race, steer, draft. Team up and chase those faster riders!

name: No Spring Chickens - Herd Masters Racing
description:
No Spring Chickens - Herd Masters Racing
No Spring Chickens is back for another round of masters racing! This is your chance to compare
yourself to riders that are the same age. It doesn’t matter if you have passed the spring chicken
stage only recently or a little longer ago, this is a race for you.
No Spring Chickens is a mass start race, all age groups will have their own starting pen, but they will
all take off together. After the finish the results for men and women will be split on ZwiftPower. That
way we hope everyone will find someone to race with and have some fun.
Series schedule:
20-22 March: Wandering Flats (25.2 km, flat, Makuri)
27-29 March: Green to Screen (28.5 km, rolling, New York)
3-5 April: Power Punches (23.7 km, repeated small climbs, Watopia)
10-12 April: Bon Voyage (31.4 km, flat to rolling, France)
17-19 April: Cobbled Crowns (24.3 km, short sharp hills, Richmond)
24-26 April:Itza Climb Finish (30 km, medium hilly finish, watopia)
Race rules:
1. No TT Bikes.
2. Steering is enabled.
3. Smarttrainer or powermeter required.
4. Heart rate monitor required.
5. No indoor bikes without controlled resistance (cadence=power).
6. Participants must be registered on zwiftpower.com to be included in the results.
7. For podium finishers in all age groups: Elite level performances above the limits stated below
must be supported by dual power recordings uploaded to Zwiftpower
○ Men: above 5w/kg or 375w for 20min / 6w/kg or 475w for 5min / 9w/kg for 1min.
○ Women: above 4.5w/kg or 280w for 20min / 5.5w/kg or 350w for 5min / 7.5w/kg for
1min.

8. For podium finishers in all age groups: Zwift activities must be saved PUBLIC for verification.

name: THE COALITION - Chase To The Underground
description:
Welcome to the ‘CHASE TO THE UNDERGROUND’ chase race series by The Coalition.

A chase race is a handicapped race whereby the lower-category riders set off first and are chased down by higher-category riders. D’s start first, with the A riders heading off last.

Each category will adorn one of the iconic COALITION kits and must work together to stand the best chance of winning. So keep the pace high, but don't attack until the very end!

The Coalition is one of the fastest-growing teams on Zwift, with over 40 teams in the Zwift Racing League. If you would like to join us, then find out more at https://coalitionracing.com

RR!

#coalition

eventSubgroups.name: THE COALITION - Chase To The Underground (A)
THE COALITION - Chase To The Underground (B)
THE COALITION - Chase To The Underground (C)
THE COALITION - Chase To The Underground (D)

name: Group Run 10k
description:
Join us for a 10k group run.   It's run-at-your-own-pace and all are welcome! 

Pick the closest pace group for you, but all groups will start together and you are free to fall back and run ahead. The pace groups designate average pace for the duration of the run. Most groups will start slower and finish faster than the indicated pace. Feel free to mix in some intervals and race your friends. #pbt

name: Stage 2: Zwift Games: Hudson Hustle
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

name: Zwift TT Club Racing - Champs Elysees
description:
The Time Trial. Cycling’s true leveler - just you and your legs versus everyone else in the field.

Come join Zwift TT Club on Zwift for more Time Trial event information! https://www.zwift.com/clubs/b3d1a903-df49-44eb-9c6f-4ba9f0622efe/home

Join us as we embark on a five-week series of events - each week you will have multiple chances to set your best time on the same route!

Over the course of the five weeks, we’ll be increasing the distance of the events.

Check out Zwiftpower.com after your event to see how well you stack up against the competition!

Don’t forget to check you’re using the fastest bike frame and wheels available to you!

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? 
For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Velos Monuments – Tour of Flanders – Spring Classics competition #6/12
description:
Following last year’s highly successful edition, we’re proud to launch the Spring Classics Competition 2026!

This 12 week series kicks off on 27 February 2026, with a race every Friday at 19:25 UTC.

Our format features:
• Mass start racing with separate standings per race category
• A time based general classification across all 12 races
• The 5 Monuments races, each counting towards a dedicated points classification
Whether you're chasing the podium, improving your fitness, or riding for fun with friends, the Velos community welcomes all motivated Zwifters.
Join us on the virtual roads and experience the thrill of the Spring Classics — Velos style!

Race 6/12
The Tour of Flanders: One of the most iconic races on the calendar!
With its cobbles, punchy climbs and draggy/flat finish, the UCI Worlds course from Richmond is the ideal terrain for our Tour of Flanders.

name: EVO CC Sprint Race Series
description:
CATEGORY ENFORCEMENT IS ENABLED FOR ALL EVO RACES!

What is Category Enforcement? 

Category Enforcement will prevent Zwifters from entering a category they are not eligible for.  Our goal is to increase fairness in racing.

HOW DOES IT WORK?

- Zwift checks a racer's past performance using VO2 max and FTP to determine their race category.
- Racers can join a category at or above their category. For example, if you are "C" category, you can race in C, B, or A category. You will not be able to race in the D category and the option to join will be absent.

Welcome to EVO sprintrace series. Shorter more compact races for the rider looking for an effective high intensity race. Start fast, hang on in the middle, then sprint for the win at the finish. Expect to push your heart rate to its maximum. Sprinkle a few of these into your training plan and watch your fitness grow.

To make racing as fair as possible we have enforced a few minor rules that will result in automatic disqualification on ZwiftPower.

All riders must wear a heart rate monitor
Riding in an incorrect category.

zPower riders will be disqualified in A, B, and C Podium. zPower is allowed in the D category. Race details and final results will be available at www.zwiftpower.com

#evo

name: Fast Friday Workout
description:
Congratulations, you crushed another week of workouts! If you’re looking to cap off a great week with another can't-miss training session, check out Fast Friday on Zwift. 

A new workout series to put in your weekly run rotation, Fast Friday is a simple and streamlined way to get your heart rate up. We’re focusing on short reps here, clocking in fast intervals that help runners increase their top speed. Short. Sharp. Fast. High-intensity intervals are proven to take your performance to the next level. So lace up and get moving for just 20–30 minutes every Friday before checking out of the office and into the weekend. 

#fastfriday

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: FRR CLASSICA SERIES - BELGICA - ITT
description:
Flamme Rouge Racing are excited to bring you our CLASSICA events to honour the great one day monuments of world cycling.

ITALIA
BELGICA
FRANCAIS

Each event will offer 3 races Friday to Sunday, using three formats - iTT / Scratch / Points - each race will convert to points, based on segments and finish - a riders points total will be drawn from all 3 races, do more, score more!

Each event will center around the type of course and terrain of the monument, with the actual race day event being of a length that is fitting for the monument.

Classica races are part of the FRR Weekend Warrior Race Series for which there will be at least 10 WW events, refer to website for the Schedule and rules:

Series Schedule
https://flammerougeracing.com/race-schedule/

Rules
https://flammerougeracing.com/race-rules

Event Basics
- Public events - MIXED RACING
- Five Max 30day ZRS ranged pens
- Mandatory racing experience - HRM - Smart Trainers


- Season long Order of Merit (OoM)
- Best 7 scoring events to count (an event counts the points from all 3 races over

FRR Registered riders form the OoM - Top 10 earn prizes on condition they are FRR Subscribers 

For all details, registration and how to become an FRR subscriber vist our website:
https://flammerougeracing.com/

Primary Communication via DISCORD

Discord - RACE channel for questions, updates and lively chat - it is by far the best place to clarify or report anything during the series

Invite link: https://discord.com/invite/nQNWHQK6PS

Smart Trainers - HRM
- Smart Trainers are mandatory
- Race Control can verify with the Zwift platform 
- HRMs are mandatory

So be SMART and use a SMART trainer as your PRIMARY power source where you can....

FRR Code of Conduct
- We expect the best racing behavior from all riders, if riders have an issue then always contact race control via email first, we will investigate every request raised.
- In race we do not expect riders to abuse others, flag, report to race control, screen shot etc.... riders who ignore this will be DQ'd for the season
- Discord chat remains objective and banter - anything more will be removed and warnings/bans will be issued

And finally remember we all do this for fun and exercise so ride on and enjoy the FRR events....stay safe and if it starts to hurt then welcome to the fun zone...! 
(But stop if it really does hurt too much please!)

name: Friday Night Fun Race
description:
Get ready for the Friday Night Fun Race – the perfect way to unwind and kick-off your weekend! This is fast, friendly, and totally unserious racing designed for pure enjoyment.

Every week brings a different course and theme, so you never know what fun-fueled surprise awaits! And if that wasn't enough, everyone starts with a 'BOOST' power-up, with extras available throughout the race – that’s a speed increase for a few seconds – use it wisely!

SCHEDULE
Races are held weekly on Friday evenings across various timezones.

CATEGORIES
You’ll be categorized by your existing Racing Score for fair competition. But don't worry – race results won't impact your score, keeping the pressure low and the fun high.

Sign up for the ultimate Friday night race – fun-filled, feel-good, and guaranteed to make your weekend start with a bang!

name: Zwift Epic Race - Downtown Dolphin
description:
Join us as we take on some slightly more challenging race routes!

For more Epic Races, please join the club on the Companion App: https://www.zwift.com/clubs/a1ea0324-7f4a-46a7-8547-a7a3246e349b/home

All racers will start together, so make sure you're ready at the start!

WHAT IS ZWIFTPOWER? 
For further information on connecting to ZwiftPower, check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H 

Please note: For your results to show up in ZwiftPower, you must use a heart rate monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter. 

Check out zwift.com/racing for more information on how you can participate in other events offered by Zwift and the community to have a great racing experience.

#zwiftofficial

name: Newbury Velo Crit
description:
Newbury Velo presents your weekly nocturnal criterium. No crashes. No marshals, No sign-on. No fee. Just 12 laps of Type2Fun.

Club results will be posted on our Facebook Page: https://www.facebook.com/newburyvelo

Riders must be registered on https://www.zwiftpower.com and have their Zwift profile agreed to share with zwiftpower otherwise they will not be included in the results. Think of it as the signing in process :) details of how to do this can be found here: https://boltrace.team/zwift-power-guide

CATEGORIES

A: 4.0-5.0 w/kg
B: 3.2-3.9 w/kg
C: 2.5 /kg-3.1 w/kg
D: 1.0- 2.49 w/kg

#newburyvelo

name: Zwift Crit Racing Club - Glasgow Crit Circuit
description:
It's Crit time! Come and join in the fun on a number of multi-lap short routes. Get ready to dig deep and push to the finish line!

What is Racing Score?

Racing Score is an intuitive system that replaces the traditional categories (A - D) with a 0-1000 scale to reflect your current level, considering various metrics like race performances and power. This score helps Zwift recommend the most competitive band to race in, ensuring a fun, rewarding, and exciting experience as you track your progression throughout your Zwift racing adventures.

WHAT IS ZWIFT POWER? For further information on connecting to Zwift Power check out this support page: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/connecting-to-zwiftpower-SyldRc_4H

Please note; for your results to show up in Zwift Power you must be using a Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) and be riding on a smart trainer, smart bike, or using a power meter.

name: Full Glaz Challenge
description:
Le B&B HOTELS p/b KTM renoue avec la compétition sur Zwift. Affrontez-vous, tous les vendredis soirs, sur dix courses virtuelles dans les mondes de Watopia, Richmond, New-York, Londres, Innsbruck, France, Yorkshire, Bologne et Paris pour décrocher la victoire. Glazic sera parmi vous pour repérer les talents ! 

The Full Glaz Challenge is back. A set of 10 races organized by the Men in Glaz and open to all Zwifters! Compete against riders from all over the World.

name: VirtuSlo 4Endurance Steady Ride
description:
Welcome to our weekly VirtuSlo 4Endurance Steady Ride. 


This is a fun social ride with the aim of improving your endurance. Stick together to enjoy the draft and companionship. Feel free to join in-ride conversation.


We will not tolerate flyers/racers - they will be removed from the ride by the one and only mrs. Fence. 

We will try to have a sweeper on each ride to help any riders with technical (or other) difficulties come back to the group. If you feel strong, please help our red beacon team. We recommend using a »late-join« option if you fall behind in the first 30 minutes of the ride.


Feel free to join all our events at https://www.zwift.com/eu/events/tag/virtuslo
 
VirtuSlo 4Endurance Schedule
More info: https://zwiftpower.com/series.php?id=VIRTUSLO


During the rides we use the group ride channel on our discord server https://discord.gg/x8CNEMRteS 
You are welcome to join for even more interaction. 


Check out our website https://virtuslo.cc/ for more info about us.


#VirtuSlo #4Endurance

name: INC RELENTLESS  Friday Fun Training Ride 3.0
description:
INC RELENTLESS welcomes you to our Fun Friday Social Group ride. Experience a social training ride with occasional pace up efforts and / or chase group.

Aiming for an average 3wkg, riders are welcome to this Innovation Cycling Training Ride event as part of the INC RELENTLESS training rides that happen daily focusing on different areas of improvement.

Our goal for the event is simple
1. To have a group ride with a chase option if you wait in the pen
2. to make your ride a great experience and support you to the end
3. to have fun

Elevate your training and share the adventure with us! Yahoo!

eventSubgroups.name: INC RELENTLESS  Friday Fun Training Ride 3.0 (B)

name: MEC Bikes NZ - Ride / Race
description:
Join us for MEC Bikes weekly ride / race! The format of this ride is based around the idea of being steady from the start and building towards a race in the last 15km. Steady meaning 2.5-3w/kg. This is a drop ride and all riders are welcome but we would love to see the group stick together for as long as possible!
For more details about our other Zwift events, check our our Facebook Group - MEC Community Zwift Rides or our website www.mec.co.nz <http://www.mec.co.nz/> - See you there! Ride On!

name: Giant Friday Funday
description:
Join some of your favorite Giant leaders for a fun, social group ride with a pace that matches the description.
It will be a great introduction to group riding with full pace instructions and group banter.
The ride will target the 1.8 to 2.2 wkg average for the entire ride. Ride pace may occasionally be higher on uphills and lower on downhills but will average within the range.
Please ride with the beacon the entire time and have fun!

name: Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This final session brings it all together. Two long tempo efforts build strong, sustainable endurance and reward the work you’ve done. Settle into a steady rhythm, stay composed, and finish the Alpecin–Premier Tech Training Camp strong!

name: Stage 3: Zwift Games: Cobbled Crown
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

name: AHDR FAB Rolls p/b JetBlack
description:
The weekend is upon us, so you know what that means - no Bacon Rolls! The time has come for the AHDR Full Aussie Brekkie Roll! Just like your favorite weekday morning treat, but bigger and better for Saturday morning. The kitchen opens at 0615 but be early as the “Full” sign goes up after 30 minutes. The buffet will cover longer courses with rolling terrain at a brisk endurance pace to satisfy your weekend craving. The kitchen will close after 2 hours but those who still have leftovers will have time to finish their course. The fence will be on, but the blob will rule the road. We will ride at around 2.5-3.0W/kg but will adjust this to the group as necessary. Please stay together as a group around the Beacon. It is a steady endurance ride, not a race. All are welcome - join us for as long as you want.

#ahdr

name: 3R True2 Steady Ride [2.0w/kg avg]
description:
This is a 3R Group Ride Event. Your co-operation and teamwork is essential to the success of the ride and we ask that no riders ride off the front of the group. The event is designed as part of 3R’s package of #ProStyleTraining events to assist with the attainment of your training goals and may include intervals over and below the ride average parameters. This is not a race so please follow the leader beacon's (Yellow) instructions and if you are feeling strong, please help the keeper beacon (Red) to assist other riders back to the pack.  We welcome good banter and aim to create a fun, social atmosphere for your training. We welcome any feedback on our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/groups/ClubWorldBicycleRelief/ #Team3R #Ride3R #RideRaceRaise.

#3r

name: TEAM TYPE ONE STYLE Friday Fun Social Ride
description:
TEAM TYPE ONE STYLE is the UK’s first multi-sport team which is formed entirely
of athletes who live, ride, run, swim, race and most importantly thrive with
Type One Diabetes. To get us all through the year, TEAM TYPE ONE STYLE proudly
hosts this weekly social event every Friday at 19:30 UK Time which is open to
all, including those with functional as well as ornamental pancreases.

At TEAM TYPE ONE STYLE, we are united in one aligned ambition…
Shifting the Perception of Type One Diabetes

Type One Diabetes may be a lifelong condition, but with good support and
education, it is in no way life-limiting. We hope to help fill the gap for our
recreational and racing athletes by offering education and support across
various channels as well as providing inspiration to all.

Whilst we can’t be cured of Type One Diabetes, we can overcome this condition!
Here at this event, we look to prove that it doesn't hamper our ability to
perform at our best and compete at the top.

In this weekly event, if you're doing 1.0w/kg or 5.0w/kg you’re welcome to join
us in this supportive environment every week and push yourself to improve. We’ll
be keeping things social and keeping the fence on from time to time to keep us
all bunched up whilst we talk all things type one diabetes, then let you loose
for some intervals or max sprints as guided by our coaches.

#TEAMTYPEONESTYLE [https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/INFLITETYPEONE]
#SHIFTINGTHEPERCEPTIONOFTYPEONEDIABETES
[https://www.zwift.com/events/tag/SHIFTINGTHEPERCEPTIONOFTYPEONEDIABETES]

eventSubgroups.name: TEAM TYPE ONE STYLE Friday Fun Social Ride  (E)

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: Friday Night Fun Race
description:
Get ready for the Friday Night Fun Race – the perfect way to unwind and kick-off your weekend! This is fast, friendly, and totally unserious racing designed for pure enjoyment.

Every week brings a different course and theme, so you never know what fun-fueled surprise awaits! And if that wasn't enough, everyone starts with a 'BOOST' power-up, with extras available throughout the race – that’s a speed increase for a few seconds – use it wisely!

SCHEDULE
Races are held weekly on Friday evenings across various timezones.

CATEGORIES
You’ll be categorized by your existing Racing Score for fair competition. But don't worry – race results won't impact your score, keeping the pressure low and the fun high.

Sign up for the ultimate Friday night race – fun-filled, feel-good, and guaranteed to make your weekend start with a bang!

name: The Saturday Shop Ride by Trek Australia
description:
Complete this ride to unlock the Trek Segafredo Training kit!

In March 2020 we turned the legendary Trek Melbourne Shop Rides into a Zwift event for "just a few weeks..." four years later we have an amazing global community who meet up every Saturday for all the banter, coffee, fast and slow fun of a social shop ride. We stop twice to regroup along the way to keep it social, and our leaders and sweepers ensure everyone who wants to can get to the end. And if 70.02km is too short, you can even stay on for some TrekStras!

If you're looking for a supportive community bunch ride, or even your first ride on Zwift this is the perfect place to Ride Bikes, Have Fun and Feel Good!

Check out our club Trek Australia New Zealand Ride Club to stay in the loop.

name: The Velos Klick - 1km Challenge
description: A klick is a military term for a distance of 1km, and so Team Velos brings you a challenge to complete 1km in the fastest time possible. We will change courses monthly (all Flat courses) and we will have winners from each category at the end of the month. Choose your bike very carefully as it could make all the difference, the challenge is available every night during the week use it as a warm up for a race or just use it to improve your 1km time or power figures, whatever just have fun.

name: WOW Course Time Attack Race on Cadex TT bike with Draft
description:
The WOW Zwift events are the fastest way to collect happy miles while meeting exceptional riders/ friends on ZWIFT. While you will be assigned the fastest TT bike (Cadex) and the latest DT Swiss Disc wheel set in draft mode, you will still be upgrading your bike of choice selected in your garage before starting the ride.

Our Ds leave the pen first and hold the group together within D power limits. Several minutes later C’s will start and keep a higher end C pace. Further several minutes later the A-B train starts unleashing their magic to make up the delay from the groups starting ahead. Can all 3 teams come together in the final segments of the ride and celebrate the ONE WOW FAMILY? All groups will see each other, transfers between the groups on the go will be possible. The ride leaders will help make those transitions smooth.

WOW is a no drama, full fun ride!

Double draft is on and the Cadex TT bikes will be drafting!

name: Rhino Racing Short Crits
description:
Fast and fun criterium races with five race times every Tuesday, Thursday, Friday, and Saturday.
A power meter or smart trainer is required for entry. A ZwiftPower account is required to be counted in the official results.
View the calendar of all Rhino Racing events: https://www.zwift.com/uk/events/tag/rhinoracing
Do you enjoy these events? We want to hear from you. Share your feedback on the Rhino Racing Discord.
Interested in team racing? We do ZRL, Dirt Racing Series, Club Ladder, and WTRL TTT events. Join us! https://discord.com/invite/wPD4HJ6

name: NPRC - Run Every Day Powered By NoblePro
description:
Come and join the fastest growing running club on Zwift.  With a choice of 5 or 10k you can pick your distance. Whether you are a beginner or experienced runner come and join us, you can run at whatever pace you like, it’s not a race unless you want it to be!

Each day is a different route and as new routes are released the courses will change to allow you to bag that badge.

You will run in our in game NPRC jersey and if you complete the event you get to keep it to wear wherever you like.

We also hold special events where you can win an actual jersey to wear in real life.

NPRC Facebook – We have a facebook group where you can join and talk about all things running.  Share your stories and get help and advice.

https://www.facebook.com/groups/1223724692417314/

NoblePro: - We are a team focused on elevating running through our treadmills and digital platforms. We create treadmills loved by runners and the wider running community for quality engineered hardware features and advanced software integration to give you the freedom to train with the apps you love.
https://www.noble-pro.com/

Kinni:- In a fitness world where treadmill bluetooth integration can be a confusing place, we aim to provide you with the ability to effortlessly connect. You can run structured treadmill workouts, connect and integrate with your favourite bluetooth fitness apps such as Zwift, Rouvy, Kinomaps and many more. We are focused on progress and advancement in integration, connectivity and control.
https://kinni.co/https://kinni.co/devices/ - To see if your treadmill is compatible to link to Zwift.

Join the NoblePro Running Club (NPRC) - https://www.zwift.com/clubs/e7829ccd-bce4-4244-9ccf-4cdc3ef148ff/home

name: The WarmUp Ride
description:
From humble beginnings on Melbourne's Mornington Peninsula, ZMCC has grown to become a favourite hangout within the Zwiftverse. Whether you're looking to push yourself, recover or just spin around - ZMCC is always there for you. Bad dad jokes and questionable banter are just extras you can't buy.

We make the following promises:

We won't yell at the flyers - let them live their imaginary life.
We won't wait for stragglers - we can't help poor event choices or tech issues.
We won't bore or tease you with pretend drinks or snacks.

Join us. Enjoy. You're welcome.

Like and join us on Facebook at https://www.facebook.com/groups/zwiftmelbournecyclingclub

name: TBR 101 - Endurance
description:
The aim of TBR 101 is to help riders achieve a century (100 km) at a nice, chilled pack pace of around 36 kmh. Many riders think that they might never get to wear the Zwift Metric Century jersey – yet by sitting in our pack, with a group of experienced riders to assist, this is achievable. We try to be friendly, encouraging and concentrate on the core group around the beacon. If you wish to average faster than 36 kmh we suggest you look at other events such as Asia 120 that commences 2 hours after TBR 101. We have lots of laughs and banter – all helping to pass the time. We don't use the fence for this ride as we believe all riders should exercise self-control and respect the ride leader by riding with the yellow beacon at all times. Your co-operation helps us provide everyone with a fun, supportive and inclusive ride. Follow TBR at thebigring.cc to learn more about our events, read ride reports and leave feedback. Spotify https://open.spotify.com/playlist/4SKGVF1M70ccToY7RA82eR?si=9nX9SZZoRW-48dEPM59riw

#TBR

name: JETT Hill Climb & Interval Ride
description:
Welcome to Japan eSports Tempting Team's Hill Climb & Interval  training!

１．The signal is on Leader's [Go].
Be sure to stay behind the leader just before the sprint & KOM.
Immediately after the sprint & Climb , please make efforts to re-group immediately (1.0w/kg at the head and 2.0 to 2.5w/kg at the back). 
Since we want to return to base pace immediately after re-grouping, your cooperation with Regroup is essential.

２．Interval Training is ... Hard time: 3.5 w / kg to 6.0 w / kg ··· 2 minutes to 5 minutes
Follow the leader's power instructions and enjoy interval training.

In addition, mini races are also planned in the final stage!
Start is on my 'go'
Be sure to stay behind the leader before you start.

Ride On !!

* Please note that if you lead with abnormal speed, flag will be set.

Hill Climb Training!
SprintとIntervalを組み合わせてトレーニングを実施しますします。

１．スプリント& KOMの合図は、私の 【 Go 】です。
スプリン& KOM直前は、必ずリーダーの後ろにいてください。
スプリント&KOM直後は、すぐにリグループするよう努力してください(先頭は1.0w/kg、後方は2.0〜2.5w/kg)。リグループ後すぐに、ベースペースへ戻したいため、リグループへの皆さんのご協力は、不可欠です。
２．インターバルトレーニングは，ハードタイム：3.5w/kg~6.0w/kg・・・2分～5分
リーダーのパワー指示に従い，インターバルトレーニングを楽しみましょう。
なお、終盤にミニレースも予定してます！！
スタートは，リーダーの「GO」が合図です。スタート前に必ずリーダーの後ろにいてください。
Ride On !!

※異常なスピードでリードした場合、フラグを立てますのでご注意下さい。

#jett #japan

name: Stage 4: Zwift Games: Peaky Pave
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

name: SROZ's The Lone Wolf Chrono
description:
Welcome to SROZ's Lone Wolf Chrono! This isn't about sitting in the wheels or playing tactics in the pack. This is a pure test of grit, pacing, and individual strength. Whether you are chasing a new Personal Best or just want to see where your fitness stands, this is your chance to empty the tank against the clock.

The Mission: One rider. One bike. Total focus.

Please ensure your weight and height are up to date. Heart Rate Monitors are required for podium contenders.

name: Fast Friday Workout
description:
Congratulations, you crushed another week of workouts! If you’re looking to cap off a great week with another can't-miss training session, check out Fast Friday on Zwift. 

A new workout series to put in your weekly run rotation, Fast Friday is a simple and streamlined way to get your heart rate up. We’re focusing on short reps here, clocking in fast intervals that help runners increase their top speed. Short. Sharp. Fast. High-intensity intervals are proven to take your performance to the next level. So lace up and get moving for just 20–30 minutes every Friday before checking out of the office and into the weekend. 

#fastfriday

name: Ride Energizer JZQc
description:
Ride énergique du vendredi,pour ceux qui veulent apprendre à bien rouler en peloton dans Zwift.
Vitesse 1,5w/kg à 1,9w/kg
La barrière "Fence" est activée et aussi le mode "Kick-Out" pour ceux qui resteront trop longtemps en avant de la barrière !

name: Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp
description:
The Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp is where the pros put in the work, and this year, you’re invited to join them. Across five weeks of structured training, plus a make-up week, you’ll ride alongside the team on Zwift—sprinting with Jasper Philipsen and Kaden Groves, building endurance with Mathieu van der Poel, and learning from head coach Kristof De Kegel. Don't miss your chance to be a part of the structure, science, and team energy that fuels the WorldTour.

Workout 5: Alpecin-Premier Tech Team Camp

This final session brings it all together. Two long tempo efforts build strong, sustainable endurance and reward the work you’ve done. Settle into a steady rhythm, stay composed, and finish the Alpecin–Premier Tech Training Camp strong!

name: Restart Esports - Happy Hour
description:
Restart Esports - instagram.com/restart.esports

1. Heart Rate Monitor (HRM) is mandatory for all participants.

2. Power Source is Mandatory for all racers. A smart trainer or smart bike should be your power source where possible. ZPower is not allowed. 

3. Dual Recording - Should always be done if you have the capability. All podium positions are expected to dual record or have at least have a recent dual recording history on their profile. Any rider posting high level performances is expected to validate their hardware with dual recordings as a minimum. 

Results WILL be amended (if required), in the interest of fair play, for all Restart Esports hosted events.

eventSubgroups.name: Restart Esports - Happy Hour (A)
eventSubgroups.description:
Join in on a new weekly race: the Restart Racing Sunday Classic.

Restart Racing is an eRacing team that likes to race hard and have fun. eSports gives us an amazing platform to better ourselves as bike racers and is a great way to make friends and enjoy ourselves. 

Each week will feature a different course ranging in distance from 50-70kms with a target finish time of around 1.5hrs. Please use ZwiftPower's Minimum Category on your profile for race entry.

Cats A & B riders require a power source (power meter or smart trainer) plus a heart rate monitor. Podium finishers in Cat A are expected to dual record and make analysis 'Public' on Zwift Power while using a direct drive trainer and power meter.
Cat C will require a power source (power meter or smart trainer) and a heart rate monitor for podium spots.

Cat A - 4.0+ (min 250W)
Cat B - 3.2 - 4.0 w/kg (min 200W)
Cat C - 2.5 - 3.2 w/kg (min 150W)
Cat D - 1.0 - 2.5 w/kg

Each week will feature a different course ranging in distance from 50-70kms with a target finish time of around 1.5hrs. Please use ZwiftPower's Minimum Category on your profile for race entry.

Cats A & B riders require a power source (power meter or smart trainer) plus a heart rate monitor. Podium finishers in Cat A are expected to dual record and make analysis 'Public' on Zwift Power while using a direct drive trainer and power meter.
Cat C will require a power source (power meter or smart trainer) and a heart rate monitor for podium spots.

Cat A - 4.0+ (min 250W)
Cat B - 3.2 - 4.0 w/kg (min 200W)
Cat C - 2.5 - 3.2 w/kg (min 150W)
Cat D - 1.0 - 2.5 w/kg

name: Stage 5: Zwift Games: Three Step Sisters
description:
The spirit of sport and the rush of racing are back for more in this year’s Zwift Games. This isn’t about the podium—it’s about taking part and giving it your all. It’s your chance to join thousands of Zwifters from every corner of the globe, line up together, and see what you’re made of.

Zwift Games celebrates everyone who takes part. It’s the shared challenge, the sense of community, and the feeling of crossing the finish line knowing you gave it your all. Whether you’re chasing a personal best, testing your limits, or just here for the fun, Zwift Games is your stage.

Crit Cade and TT Bonus Stages

Now for the first time, you’ll have a chance to compete against other Zwifters in a gamified race that’s all about steering to pick your line, timing your PowerUps, and hitting boost pads to get ahead. Please note: for the Crit Cade Bonus Stage, a steering-enabled device is required.

In the mood for something more traditional? Hit up the Epiloch TT Bonus Stage to race against the clock through the Scottish Highlands’ punchy hills—no steering-enabled device required!

Unlocks & Rewards

1 Stage = Oakley Sphaera™ Strike Glasses
3 Stages = Zwift Games Kit
5 Stages = Oakley Velo Stelvio™ Helmet
6 Stages = 6000 XP

Zwift Games 2026 GC

Looking for extra motivation? The Zwift Games GC (General Classification) tracks your results across the five main racing stages. Each completed stage adds to your overall GC time, while individual stage standings and separate Bonus Stage rankings let you measure yourself in different ways — with only the five main stages counting toward the overall GC.

VIEW RESULTS: www.zwift.com/racing/leagues/zwiftgames2026

Racing Score

Zwift Racing Score is aimed at pairing you with riders of similar racing abilities to keep racing fun, fair, and competitive. Read the FAQ here: https://support.zwift.com/en_us/racing-score-faq-BkG9_Rqrh

ADVANCED CATEGORY = 650+
CATEGORY RANGE 1 = 0-159 | 160-269 | 270-389 | 390-509 | 510-649
CATEGORY RANGE 2 = 0-209 | 210-329 | 330-449 | 450-569 | 570-724

For riders with a Racing Score of 650 or higher, the' Advanced' category will have separate events scheduled every other hour.

Please note that to enter the Advanced category, you will need the following:
Heart Rate Monitor
Zwift Racing Score of over 650
Smart Trainer/Bike or Power Meter

Stage 1
Route: Kaze Kicker
Date: Feb 16–22

Description: Kick things off with a fast, punchy sprint on Kaze Kicker. A perfect opener to find your legs and feel the race buzz.

Stage 2
Route: Hudson Hustle 
Date: Feb 23–Mar 1 

Description: Stage 2 hits Hudson Hustle with sharp turns and sprint energy. Stay alert, hold position, and finish strong.

Stage 3
Route: Cobbled Crown 
Date: Mar 2–8

Description: Punchy climbs and classic racing vibes await on Cobbled Crown.

Stage 4
Route: Peaky Pavé
Date: Mar 9–15

Description: Time to climb. Peaky Pavé rewards patience, pacing, and strong legs as the Games start to heat up.

Stage 5
Route: Three Step Sisters
Date: Mar 16–22

Description: Go big to finish strong. Three Step Sisters brings an epic test to the finale.

Bonus Stages
Route: The Epiloch (Time Trial) OR Bell Lap (Crit Cade*)

Date: Ride either or both March 23–29
*Steering-enabled device required for Crit-Cade

name: Betty Designs Down Under
description: Betty Designs Down Under is a Saturday cruise of Watopia.  We all love to race but the Betty Designs Down Under ride is for looking stylish and cruising Watopia with friends. Starting at an easy pace and building to finish steady whilst exploring the hills of Watopia (2-3 w/kg). This ride will unlock the Betty Designs Jersey. All welcome.

name: The Friday Five Run
description: Finish your week off with a friendly run of 5 miles to propel you into the weekend. Join in and run what you can! Each subgroup will be led by a pace bot at the advertised pace.

name: RO4H Weekend Wander
description:
Welcome to the RO4H Weekend Wander.

The RO4H Weekend Wander is a social group ride hosted by the Ride On 4 Health community, where a friendly, casual, supportive, and respectful atmosphere embraces everyone from the most seasoned Zwifter to the first-time group ride participant.

This ride uses Zwift’s “Keep Everyone Together” (Rubberbanding) feature. Rubberbanding keeps everyone in the group together by adjusting in-game speed so riders of different power levels stay together. Your actual watts are still recorded, but speed and segment times won’t reflect normal in-game physics. Just ride at a comfortable effort and enjoy staying with the group from start to finish.

So come along for the RO4H Weekend Wander — you'll almost certainly break a sweat, undoubtedly hear a few dad jokes, and might even have some fun.

name: Stage 3 - The Zwift Big Spin
description:
The Zwift Big Spin is back, and adventure is calling. From Fuego Flats to the Mayan Jungle and beyond, it’s time to saddle up and explore far and wide across four epic stages in Watopia. Spin the wheel at every finish line and hunt for hidden spinners along the way. Everything from iconic Big Spin favorites to brand new prizes are up for grabs. What will you win when you explore The Big Spin? There’s only one way to find out: Ride! Spin! Win!

Stage 3
Route: Southern Coast Cruise
Date: March 30 - April 5


For Stage 3, we’re headed to Southern Coast Cruise—it’s not called the Zwift Big Spin for nothing!

name: The QueenBee Weekender
description:
Join the QueenBee Ladies Weekender group ride to kickstart your weekend the right way. The Weekender is a ‘change up’ ride format, where each week will be a new adventure; social cruises, badge hunts, elevation gains, flats, workouts and more… 
Each ride is banded, therefore no matter how hard you personally want to work, everyone stays together! 

The QueenBees are a group of women who share a love of cycling. Just as a hive works together to bring out the best in every member of the community, so do we. We strive to support, empower and encourage women to live their best lives, no matter where they are on their life, cycling or fitness journey. 

Join our QueenBee hive here (ladies only):

https://www.facebook.com/groups/218570110355444

https://www.zwift.com/clubs/37b38ab1-73e8-46ec-9d31-8ecbd903a489/home
